data.title: Early Adalimumab Induction for Treatment of Steroid Dependent Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor Associated Inflammatory Arthritis: A Pragmatic Randomized Clinical Trial
data.brief_summary: This study will examine the effectiveness of administering adalimumab as a treatment for patients in the early stages of steroid-dependent immune checkpoint Inhibitor associated inflammatory arthritis (ir-IA). Adalimumab (ADA) is a TNF inhibitor (TNFi) that is well established as a standard of care treatment for numerous types of inflammatory arthritis. It is hoped that adalimumab at the early stages of the ir-IA will reduce the symptoms and therefore reduce the need for steroids. This study is

data.title: Short Term Study on the Effect of a Fixed Dose of 12.5 mg of Prednisone as Starting Dose in Polymyalgia Rheumatica
data.brief_summary: Polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) is a common inflammatory condition affecting elderly people and involving the girdles. The mainstay of treatment is oral glucocorticoids (GC), with the recent BSR-BHPR guidelines suggesting an initial prednisone dose comprised between 15 and 20 mg as appropriate. However, probably because of the dramatic response of PMR to GC, randomized controlled trials of treatment are lacking. As a result, there is no evidence from controlled studies on the efficacy of different
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* patients with PMR, diagnosed according to the criteria of Bird et al

Exclusion Criteria:

* patients with arthritis
* patients with giant cell arteritis
* patients with Parkinson's disease
* patients with hypothyroidism
* patients with scapulohumeral periarthritis
* patients
endpoints: clinical remission within one month from the treatment initiation

data.title: To Demonstrate the Ability of Abatacept in Comparison to Placebo to Obtain a Low Disease Activity [PMR-AS (CRP) Lower or Equal to 10] Without GCs (Prednisone or Prednisolone) at Week 12 in Early Onset PMR Patients.
data.brief_summary: Polymyalgia rheumatic (PMR) is a frequent inflammatory disease. It affects the elderly, with peak incidences at the age of 70 to 80 years; an age \>50 years or older, is considered a criterion for the diagnosis. Polymyalgia rheumatica occurs at a frequency that is 3 to 10 times that of giant-cell arteritis. Disease risk varies according to race and geographic region. The incidence is highest among whites in northern European populations (about 20 cases per 100,000 persons older than 50 years of
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Age older than 50 years
* Fulfilling ACR/EULAR criteria
* Disease duration≤6 months
* No steroid since 2 weeks prior randomization
* PMR-AS≥ 17
* Absence of signs or symptoms of other musculoskeletal or connective tissue conditions
* Able to give informed consent
* Concomitant

data.title: Efficacy of Abatacept in Inflammatory Polyarthritis of Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)
data.brief_summary:
This research trial is for patients who have been diagnosed with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with swollen, tender joints (which is called inflammatory polyarthritis) because of the SLE.

The purpose of this clinical research study is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of treatment with abatacept (Abatacept) 125mg injected subcutaneously (under the skin) weekly for 16 weeks versus placebo injections(a substance with no active ingredients and therefore may have no treatment benefit) i
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Meet at least 4 of the 11 American College of Rheumatology (ACR) 1997 criteria for classification of SLE (see Appendix 1).OR meet the recent classification recommended by SLICC (Appendix 2) 6
2. ≥3 swollen and tender joints on 2 examinations at least 2 weeks apart and no more
endpoints: Number of Participants With at Least a 20% Improvement From Baseline in Tender and Swollen 28 Joint Count
